•Per-run settings: number of photons (such as 1,000), poor data

Limit (default is 11%).
•Channel settings: how often the signal is lost, how much data
is mixed, how often the detector picks up false signals and
A model for timing errors.
•Eve/Attack Settings: type of attack (such as intercept
making and retransmitting, or mixing some data, or in a signal
scramble), how much data was attacked (from 0 to 1), and the attack
When it starts and when it ends if it suits.
•Simulation Tool: Which tool to use for simulation, eg
Classical simulators in compressed air, or [7], [11], [13],
A special tool for large scale experiments inspired by [14], [21].
